Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed after pressing RIGHT. Let me compare the before and after frames to see what changed. It looks like the blue/sky blue blocks on the right end of the top bar swapped positions - the blue block moved right and the sky blue block moved left. Let me verify this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== CHANGED ROWS ===")
for i in range(len(before)):
    if before[i] != after[i]:
        print(f"Row {i}:")
        print(f"  BEFORE: {before[i][35:45]}")
        print(f"  AFTER:  {after[i][35:45]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
=== CHANGED ROWS ===
Row 19:
  BEFORE: ggggbbSSBB
  AFTER:  ggggSSbbBB
Row 20:
  BEFORE: ggggbbSSBB
  AFTER:  ggggSSbbBB
Row 21:
  BEFORE: ggggbbSSBB
  AFTER:  ggggSSbbBB
Row 22:
  BEFORE: ggggbbSSBB
  AFTER:  ggggSSbbBB

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So pressing RIGHT swapped the blue (b) and sky blue (S) blocks on the right end of the top bar. The pattern changed from `bbSS` to `SSbb`. So the blue block moved to the right of the sky blue block.
Let me understand this better. The top bar has:
- Left: blue block (b) with sky blue (S) interior
- Middle: gray bar
- Right: blue (b) and sky blue (S) blocks that can swap
Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if they swap back, and also try LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try RIGHT again to see the pattern
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
